test_objectives: To ensure the VWO Login Dashboard meets functional, security, performance, and usability requirements as defined in the PRD, providing a seamless and secure login experience for all user types.
scope: In scope: Authentication flow, input validation, password management, UI/UX design, accessibility, branding, security mechanisms, error handling, integration points, and performance under load. Out of scope: Post-login features, backend analytics engine, third-party service logic outside of integration touchpoints, and mobile app-specific functionality not related to the web dashboard.
functional_test_scenarios: Verify successful login with valid credentials
Verify error message display on invalid username or password
Test multi-factor authentication flow if enabled
Ensure session timeout after inactivity period
Verify 'Remember Me' feature persists login across sessions
Check proper redirection post-login based on user role
Test login attempts lockout mechanism after maximum retries
Validate input fields accept only expected character sets and lengths
Confirm logout functionality clears session data completely
non_functional_test_scenarios: Measure page load time for login dashboard under various network conditions
Conduct accessibility audit using WCAG 2.1 guidelines
Perform cross-browser compatibility testing on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge
Evaluate responsiveness across desktop, tablet, and mobile screen sizes
Assess UI consistency with brand guidelines and visual design standards
Run stress tests to evaluate system behavior at peak concurrent user loads
Verify compliance with GDPR and other applicable data protection regulations
Test recovery flow when login services are temporarily unavailable
api_test_scenarios: Validate authentication API returns correct response codes for valid/invalid requests
Test rate limiting implementation on login endpoints
Verify token generation and expiration policies via API calls
Check encryption standards applied to sensitive data transmitted through APIs
Test integration points with identity providers or SSO systems
Ensure secure headers and CORS policies are enforced in API responses
risk_analysis: Key risks include potential security vulnerabilities such as brute force attacks or session hijacking, performance bottlenecks during high traffic periods, inconsistent user experience across devices or browsers, non-compliance with regulatory standards like GDPR, and integration failures with external identity systems. These will be mitigated through robust test coverage, automated scanning tools, and continuous monitoring.
test_data_requirements: Need diverse set of test users including active, locked, expired accounts; sample passwords meeting complexity rules; mock third-party service responses; simulated high-load conditions; accessibility evaluation tools; and representative device/browser combinations for compatibility testing.
entry_criteria: PRD must be finalized and approved; development environment accessible with core authentication module deployed; test environments configured with necessary tools and access permissions granted; initial set of test cases documented and reviewed.
exit_criteria: All critical and high severity defects resolved; minimum 90% test case execution coverage achieved; performance benchmarks met; security scan passed without critical findings; stakeholder sign-off obtained on key functionalities and overall user experience.
